summarization.a: The figure shows the X-ray diffraction patterns of the CoO powder, before and after Hacac exposure. Different crystal planes are highlighted.

data_extraction.a:
| 2 Theta (degrees) | CoO after Hacac | CoO control |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 30 | peak at 32° | - |
| 35 | peak at 36° | peak at 36° |
| 40 | peak at 42° | peak at 42° |
| 45 | peak at 45° | - |
| 50 | - | - |
| 55 | peak at 59° | - |
| 60 | peak at 61° | peak at 61° |
| 65 | peak at 66° | - |

a.question: Which crystal planes can be seen for the CoO control powder?
a.answer: The (111), (200), and (220) crystal planes correspond to c-CoO.

a.question: Which species can be observed in the CoO powder after the Hacac exposure?
a.answer: The following ones can be observed: c-CoO and c-Co3O4.

a.question: Which crystal planes can be observed after the Hacac exposure?
a.answer: The following crystal planes can be observed: (220), (111), (200), (400), (511), (220), and (440).

a.question: What effect does the Hacac exposure have on the oxidation of the CoO powder?
a.answer: After the Hacac exposure, the c-CoO turns into c-Co3O4.
